title: quercus lanuginosa var. pusilla
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. pusilla, also known as the dwarf or small-leaved Quercus lanuginosa, is a small shrub or tree species within the Quercus lanuginosa plant family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Quercus lanuginosa var. pusilla include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to note that the plant's safety and efficacy have not been extensively studied in humans.
